← the Atlas

Remote terminals — the finale (R9–R10)

budding·proposed·JUN 29, 2026

The R9–R10 plan of record. R8's reader-join stays. The awareness architecture for remote terminals is SETTLED in the awareness design note — split observing from remembering. A memoryless producer per host emits TerminalSnapshot events, and kolu alone folds them, owns the memory (on kolu.authored), and stamps recency with its own clock. Two earlier directions are dead — the local-pulam-PROCESS (#1614, closed) and createPulam / share-the-assembly (superseded). So R9·lib dissolves. The remaining work is re-sequenced under a hard constraint (a remote tile must be as complete as a local tile, nothing deferred) into three parallel local-preserving prep PRs then one feature. Phases — S1 producer · S2 kolu-fold · R9.0 in-process · R9.1 resolver (all shipped) · PR-1 lifecycle · PR-2 fs/git · PR-3 awareness · F-REMOTE the complete remote tile · R10 canvas.

Contents

  • Roadmap
  • What R9 reuses — and what it must build
  • The sub-phases
    • S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation, and why R9·lib dissolves
    • R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds — shipped
    • R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped
  • R9 re-sequenced — prep-first, F-REMOTE complete-everything
    • PR-1 — lifecycle honors HostLocation (prep · local no-op)
    • PR-2 — fs/git & file-IO ready for any host (prep · local no-op)
    • PR-3 — awareness ready for any host (prep · local no-op)
    • F-REMOTE — the complete remote tile (the one user-visible feature; nothing deferred)
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • Open decisions

The last leg of #951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8 (#1594) shipped the right reader-join (a terminal is kolu.authored ⋈ terminalWorkspace.snapshots, joined at the reader) — keep that. The question this note settles is how kolu gets the awareness half for a remote host, and that architecture is now decided: split observing from remembering. A memoryless producer runs beside each host’s kaval and emits TerminalSnapshot events; kolu alone folds them, owns the memory (on kolu.authored), and stamps recency with its own clock. The full types/API/flows are in the awareness design; this note is the phase plan of record. The parent remote-terminals keeps the one-line rows; the model is in the terminal model.

The settled design — and the two directions it replaced

The awareness boundary is observe vs. remember (the awareness design, outcome of a grounded 3-agent debate). It supersedes two earlier attempts, both now dead: (1) the local-pulam-PROCESS (#1614, CLOSED) — it conflated a process boundary with code decoupling and introduced a fold-clobber intrinsic to mirroring an ephemeral process; (2) createPulam / “share the assembly” — it shared the wrong thing (the assembly still fused deriving with storing, so the foot-gun and clobber rode along to every consumer). The converged fix doesn’t share the assembly; it deletes it: the producer is memoryless (it can’t even spell memory, by type), so there is nothing to clobber and nothing to assemble. R9·lib therefore dissolves (below).

Roadmap

FINALE — R9 (converged) · R10✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓S1 · memoryless awareness producer — emits observations, no seed, TerminalSnapshot onlyshipped #1626 · → awareness design
✓S2 · kolu's fold owns memory — typed patches · kolu's clock · memory→authored · event streamshipped #1626
✕R9·lib · DISSOLVES — createPulam unneeded; producer + fold are pure leavessubsumed by S1+S2
✓R9.0 · kolu runs the producer in-process, folds onto authored + snapshotsshipped #1626 (foundation assessed transport-agnostic for R9.3)
✓R9.1 · the TerminalEndpoint resolver (orthogonal — shipped)#1603
◑PR-1 · lifecycle honors HostLocation — create · restore · sleep/wake · boot-adoption (prep · local no-op)in PR #1637 · ∥ PR-2 PR-3
○PR-2 · fs/git + file-IO host-aware — Code-tab procedure+pulse · byte primitives (prep · local no-op)needs R9.1 · ∥ PR-1 PR-3
○PR-3 · awareness host-ready — framer + terminalEvents wire (prep · local no-op)needs S2 · ∥ PR-1 PR-2
○F-REMOTE · the complete remote tile — PTY · fs/git · FULL awareness · restore · sleep/wake · paste/upload · transcript (nothing deferred)needs PR-1 PR-2 PR-3 · R7
○R10 · canvas multiplex + host picker + validateneeds F-REMOTE

S1 + S2 + R9.0 + R9.1 shipped (#1626, #1603) — the memoryless producer, kolu’s fold, the in-process local awareness, and the HostLocation resolver. What remains (the rows below R9.1) was re-sequenced under a hard constraint — a remote tile must be as complete as a local tile, nothing deferred — into three parallel local-preserving prep PRs (PR-1 lifecycle · PR-2 fs/git · PR-3 awareness) → F-REMOTE (the one complete-everything feature) → R10 (host-picker UX). The old R9.2/R9.3/R9.4/R9.5 rows dissolved into that shape — see R9 re-sequenced below. (Full debate record + the adversarial-verification pass: debates/r92-r93/.)

What R9 reuses — and what it must build

Shipped seams (reuse as-is):

seam where leg it serves
the per-terminal sensors @kolu/terminal-workspace the derivation S1 turns into a memoryless producer (git · pr · agent · foreground)
the pulam daemon pulam (R4) the one producer per host — now serving an observation stream, kept for the remote case
frontDaemonOverStdio @kolu/surface-daemon dial a remote kaval over ssh stdio → the remote PTY
getHostSession @kolu/surface-nix-host a pooled, long-lived session to dial a remote producer → its event stream
mirrorRemoteSurface @kolu/surface the total dual ({ procedures, done }, R7) — mirror a remote producer’s stream into one local handle
HostLocation remote arm · persisted location @kolu/common { kind:"remote", hostId }, round-trips to disk
the reader-join (composeTerminalMetadata) @kolu/common · client authored ⋈ snapshots at read + at disk — unchanged (memory just moves to the authored half)

Must build:

gap today the phase
make the sensors a memoryless producer (observations, no seed, TerminalSnapshot) the engine mutates a host record + reads it back (sensors.ts:139-162) S1
kolu’s fold owns the gates + the clock; memory moves to kolu.authored; a terminalEvents stream kolu/daemon each hand-roll a sink that writes the store S2
kolu runs the producer in-process and folds kolu wires startAwareness against a registry-aliased record R9.0
lifecycle (create · restore · sleep/wake · boot-adoption) resolves through HostLocation hard-pinned local at those seams PR-1
fs/git + file-IO (Code tab · byte preview · paste/upload · transcript) host-aware koluSurface value-streams off localEndpoint; preview byte route reads kolu-server’s local fs PR-2
awareness host-ready (framer + terminalEvents wire) so a remote stream folds through the same fold events consumed in-process, never on the wire PR-3
the remote tile itself — the ssh driver + every remote arm lit only {kind:"local"} exists F-REMOTE

The sub-phases

The awareness types, API, and interaction diagrams are specified in full in the awareness design — the awareness internals (the framer, the fold, the consumer-arm recency rule, HostTerminalKey) that PR-3 and F-REMOTE wire up. The shipped phases (S1 · S2 · R9.0 · R9.1) and the re-sequenced remaining phases (PR-1 · PR-2 · PR-3 · F-REMOTE · R10) are detailed here.

S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation, and why R9·lib dissolves

S1· memoryless awareness producer — observations, no seed, TerminalSnapshot only○ todo
needs ← R6 · R4
S2· kolu's fold owns memory — kolu's clock, memory→authored○ todo
needs ← S1

Converged + landed. The S1/S2 model below was simplified across a long review and shipped by the awareness-derive-store cutover — read that note for the final types. The summary here is updated to match: the three-category {cache, live, memory} patch model and the per-event fromSnapshot flag were dropped (snapshot-vs-delta rides the frame, not the event).

S1 rewrites the sensors into a memoryless producer: it emits per-field TerminalEvent events, holds no durable memory, takes no seed, and its export type TerminalSnapshot (the five re-observable fields) cannot spell the memory fields. It deletes the AwarenessSink, both makeAwarenessSink impls, the AwarenessRecord, and the apply-and-publish read-back contract. S2 gives kolu the only fold: fold(cur, o, ctx) → TerminalState ({snapshot, memory}), last-write-wins for the five snapshot fields, stamping recency with kolu’s clock on a live agent-identity change and remembering lastAgentCommand (the resume target rides the authored record as the fold-derived discriminated restoreTarget). It writes memory onto kolu.authored — so every snapshots collection everywhere is TerminalSnapshot.

Why R9·lib dissolves: createPulam was an attempt to share the assembly the daemon and kolu each hand-rolled. Under S1+S2 there is no assembly — the producer and the fold are pure leaves, each home’s loop is ~8 lines, and the producer can’t write memory, so there is nothing to clobber and nothing to consolidate. The R9·lib / createPulam phase is removed.

  • Needs: R6 (the sensor library) and R4 (the pulam daemon) — both shipped.
  • Done (S1): the producer emits observations and touches no host store; AwarenessSink/makeAwarenessSink×2/AwarenessRecord/seed are gone. Done (S2): kolu’s fold is the sole writer of memory (onto kolu.authored); the write-fence, clock authority, and single-memory-writer are enforced by type (tests in the awareness note). The remote terminalEvents wire stream is R9.3; R9.0 folds the in-process producer and pulam serves a TerminalSnapshot collection.

R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds — shipped #1626

R9.0· kolu runs the memoryless producer in-process and folds✓ shipped
needs ← S2links #1626

kolu’s local awareness is the memoryless producer called in-process (no daemon, no socket, no mirror), its observations fed straight into kolu’s fold. kolu seeds current from its durable record, folds with its clock, writes the memory fields to kolu.authored and the rest to the snapshots collection. The reader-join is unchanged (composeTerminalMetadata now reads memory from the authored half). Clobber-free by type (the producer can’t spell memory). Hardened by a 4-reviewer architecture-first-principles + perfection-review gauntlet (the fold-produced RestoreTarget, value-based recency, the publish-boundary guard); the type vocabulary is TerminalSnapshot · TerminalEvent · TerminalState · Known<T>. A 4-reviewer foundation debate then assessed it transport-agnostic — R9.3 reuses the fold and producer verbatim (debates/r93-foundation/).

  • Migration: a hard cutover, no flag/fallback; plus the memory→kolu.authored schema move (SCHEMA_VERSION bump + state migration).
  • Shipped: kolu runs no bespoke sink/assembly; local awareness is the producer + kolu’s fold; reader-join + disk persistence hold; differential tests pass; CI green.

R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped #1603

R9.1· HostLocation-keyed endpoint resolver✓ shipped
needs ← R8links #1603

The PTY-endpoint path — orthogonal to awareness, so the redesign doesn’t touch it. resolveTerminalEndpoint is the one HostLocation-keyed seam mapping a terminal to its kaval endpoint; the {kind:"remote"} arm fails loudly until R9.2 fills it (.exhaustive() forces a deliberate arm). Per-terminal ops resolve off entry.meta.location, so R9.2 is a one-case addition.

R9 re-sequenced — prep-first, F-REMOTE complete-everything

The original R9.2 → R9.3 → R9.4 → R9.5 order shipped a remote tile in pieces (PTY first; Code tab, restore, awareness bolted on later) — which violates the hard constraint settled across the debates/r92-r93/ rounds and ratified by an adversarial-verification workflow: no PR may break existing functionality, and a remote tile must be as complete as a local tile the moment a user can create one — nothing deferred. So R9 re-sequences into three parallel, local-preserving prep PRs (each makes one concern host-aware as a no-op on local) → F-REMOTE (the one PR that lights up a complete remote tile) → R10 (host-picker UX). R9.2/R9.3/R9.4 dissolve into this shape; R9.5 becomes prep (PR-2); R-pulamweb-4 leaves kolu’s critical path (kolu mirrors the remote pulam server-side via R7, not the pulam-web browser leg).

Why three prep PRs, not six (decompose by concern, not by seam)

HostLocation is already the source of truth for “which host” (R9.1) — so completing it is one idea per concern (the lifecycle, fs/git + file-IO, awareness), not one PR per file touched. Each prep PR is a pure local no-op; all three are logically independent → fully parallel — PR-2 reads the already-persisted entry.meta.location, it does not need PR-1 to set it. The only coordination is a surface-version rebase between PR-2 and PR-3 (both bump TERMINAL_WORKSPACE_CONTRACT_VERSION). The adversarial pass also recovered three first-class capabilities silently pinned to the local host — sleep/wake, paste/upload, transcript export — now first-class in the prep + F-REMOTE (each routed to its host, never silently wrong-host).

PR-1 — lifecycle honors HostLocation (prep · local no-op)

PR-1· lifecycle honors HostLocation — create · restore · sleep/wake · boot-adoption▶ do next
needs ← R9.1links #1637

Thread location through every remaining lifecycle seam so the terminal lifecycle resolves through HostLocation (finishing what R9.1 started): create (createTerminal / the create RPC / handleCreate, default LOCAL_LOCATION, spawn via resolveTerminalEndpoint(location)); restore (stop dropping t.location, useSessionRestore.ts); sleep/wake — extend TerminalEndpoint with sleep/releaseSleptPty/wake (local arm = today’s helpers verbatim) and route sleepTerminal/wake by entry.meta.location, exactly as kill/attach already do; boot-adoption — host-key the ptyHost singletons into a map (one local entry today; parameterize adoptSurvivingSession/reconcile per host, location-parameterize the orphan-adopt).

  • Why whole: every record is {kind:"local"} today → every resolve returns localTerminalEndpoint → create / restore / sleep / wake / adoption are byte-identical. No user-facing change.
  • Needs: R9.1. Parallel with PR-2 · PR-3.
  • Done: sleepWake.test.ts + restore + boot-adoption green unchanged; a saved location round-trips create→restore.

PR-2 — fs/git & file-IO ready for any host (prep · local no-op)

PR-2· fs/git + file-IO host-aware — Code-tab procedure+pulse · byte primitives○ todo
needs ← R9.1

Make everything a terminal reads or writes on disk resolve to its host. Rewrite the Code tab off koluSurface’s value-bearing streams onto the shared surface’s procedure + {seq} pulse (CodeTab.tsx; wire.ts already holds the workspace client; the two wire shapes are in surface live data) — reproduce the .use().pending() selection-stability guard, relocate the binary-preview/iframe orchestration client-side. Resolve the server fs/git per entry.meta.location (a per-host re-served mirror; inputs stay bare repoPath/filePath), and make the preview byte route a per-location byte source. Add three byte primitives to terminalWorkspaceSurface (3.0 → 3.1, additive): a range-capable preview-read (bytes + content-type + content-range), a scratch-write (paste/upload), a transcript-source read — each re-enforcing the realpath/traversal guard on the producing host. Route paste/upload (saveTerminalFile) + transcript export per location. (This is the old R9.5, pulled to prep — it no longer needs R-pulamweb-4 or awareness. Three serve sites: kolu-server local arm · pulam live arm · pulam-web forwards each. The remote arms are throwing stubs lit by F-REMOTE.)

  • Why whole: every git mode, diff, file read, image/PDF/HTML preview (incl. the ../%2f/symlink-escape 403) behaves as today; the new byte procedures serve in-process locally. No user-facing change (Code tab byte-identical).
  • Needs: R9.1. Parallel with PR-1 · PR-3 (shares a surface-version rebase with PR-3).
  • Done: Code tab byte-identical incl. preview; the three byte procedures serve locally; surface.test.ts pinned at 3.1.

PR-3 — awareness ready for any host (prep · local no-op)

PR-3· awareness host-ready — framer + terminalEvents wire○ todo
needs ← S2

Get awareness into the right shape + a pipe so a remote host’s sensors can feed kolu’s same fold. Build a TerminalFrame type + framer (deriving phase/seq only; it stamps every local emission phase:"delta"); route kolu’s local producer through it. The live decision — (phase === "delta") && agentIdentityChanged(baseline, value), skip "unknown" — and the durable recencyBaseline live in kolu’s consumer arm (NOT the framer, NOT fold — fold.ts verbatim), extracted as a shared, re-seedable unit F-REMOTE reuses. Add TerminalEventSchema + TerminalFrameSchema (zod) + the terminalEvents stream on terminalWorkspaceSurface (3.1 → 3.2, additive); pulam serves it live beside its snapshots cache (emitting the commandRun marks the cache drops); kolu-server quiet-defaults it (its local in-process fold untouched); pulam-web forwards it.

  • Why whole: a differential test pins local fold output (esp. lastActivityAt) equal pre/post and asserts the fresh-spawn recency bump is preserved + no-bump on adopt/sleep-wake (the adopt guard — adoptedSnapshot seeds agent:null, so only the seeded baseline’s live=false prevents a spurious bump). A served-but-unconsumed terminalEvents is real, tested producer capability. No user-facing change.
  • Needs: S2 (shipped). Parallel with PR-1 · PR-2.
  • Done: local awareness byte-identical; a terminalEvents subscriber gets snapshot-then-commandRun-delta; pulam-tui/web unaffected; surface.test.ts pinned at 3.2.

F-REMOTE — the complete remote tile (the one user-visible feature; nothing deferred)

F-REMOTE· remote terminal — PTY · fs/git · full awareness · restore · sleep/wake · paste/upload · transcript○ todo
needs ← PR-1 · PR-2 · PR-3 · R7

RemoteTerminalEndpoint fills resolveTerminalEndpoint’s {kind:"remote",hostId} arm and lights every remote arm the prep created, on the ONE remote-pulam mirror it already needs. A remote tile becomes a full peer to a local one:

  • PTY over the remote kaval (getHostSession({binary:"kaval"}); remote spawn-env from the host’s system.info, inverting the local cleanEnv()-wins layering).

  • Real fs/git Code tab + the host-aware binary-preview byte route over the remote pulam (R7 mirror; PR-2’s byte primitives; realpath/traversal guards re-enforced on the host; the HTML-artifact decorator fed remote bytes).

  • Full awareness — subscribe terminalEvents on the same mirror, run PR-3’s shared consumer arm, fold via the same fold, keyed by a branded HostTerminalKey before kolu’s aggregate (the wire stays bare TerminalId). Agent badge + recency + restore-target.

  • Session restore incl. boot re-dial-re-adopt — a new F-REMOTE loop reads saved remote hostIds, dials each host’s kaval and pulam at boot, reconciles per host; the client restore loop dials+adopts, not respawns (closes the respawn-vs-surviving-PTY race). Blip-survival is free from HostSession’s reconnect + the durable kaval --stdio daemon.

  • kill/attach (already location-routed); sleep/wake on the host; paste/upload to the host (PR-2’s scratch-write); transcript from the host (PR-2’s transcript-read).

  • The create knob (“On SSH host…” free-text → location:{kind:"remote",hostId}; works because the drv-map is arch-keyed, so any ssh-reachable, Nix-provisionable host dials like kaval-tui --host), the ssh badge + a discoverability tip, the remote-hostId daemonStatus writer, and the Nix wiring (@kolu/surface-nix-host + both drv maps).

  • Why whole: a remote tile matches a local tile on every axis — nothing deferred, nothing wrong-host. Every prep was a local no-op, so all local e2e stay green. (Optional UI-only split: complete-but-invisible endpoint → then the create knob.)

  • Needs: PR-1 · PR-2 · PR-3 · R9.1 · R7. Not R-pulamweb-4; not any further contract bump.

  • Done: an e2e creates a remote tile and asserts EVERY local-tile axis — PTY echo; Code-tab git/diff/tree + binary preview over real remote fs/git; live agent/recency/restore badges folded via the same fold; kill/attach; sleep/wake, paste/upload, transcript on the host; kolu-restart re-adopt; reload re-dial+adopt. All local e2e green.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← F-REMOTE

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front. (F-REMOTE’s create knob is the minimal “type a host”; R10 is the curated picker + multiplex.)

  • Needs: F-REMOTE.
  • Done: the validated UX call, recorded.

Open decisions

The awareness forks the debate raised are decided (recorded in the awareness design): memory home = kolu.authored; recency = identity-only (the restore-caveat heuristic deleted); dashboards = urgency-only; aggregation key = (HostLocation, TerminalId); the activity-definition predicate is fixed in one tested function. The phasing forks are decided (debates/r92-r93/): three parallel prep PRs → F-REMOTE → R10; R-pulamweb-4 cut from kolu’s path; sleep/wake · paste/upload · transcript routed per host (not silently local). The only implementation decision still open:

decision needed by options
reconnect re-validation semantics (folded into F-REMOTE’s boot re-dial-re-adopt) F-REMOTE what’s compared (location vs the remote kaval’s live cwd) + the fail-fast behavior

Referenced by

  • Awareness — the producer observes, kolu remembers
  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • The Terminal Model — two collections, one reader-side join (and what to name them)
